title: Flavourful Clay Pot Malaysian Lamb Curry
description: A rich, jammy, and shaggy Malaysian curry featuring tender lamb loin chops slow-cooked in a rustic clay pot lined with an agave leaf for an adventurous, paleo-friendly depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: lamb loin chops
ingredients.notes: bone-in, trimmed of excess hard fat

ingredients.name: shallots
ingredients.notes: peeled and finely minced to create a shaggy texture

ingredients.name: garlic and ginger paste
ingredients.notes: equal parts fresh garlic and ginger ground together

ingredients.name: lemongrass stalks
ingredients.notes: bruised and bottom thirds finely minced

ingredients.name: malaysian curry powder
ingredients.notes: paleo-friendly blend of coriander, cumin, fennel, and turmeric

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: for searing the lamb and cooking the aromatics

ingredients.name: agave leaf
ingredients.notes: washed, spines removed, and softened over a flame to line the clay pot

instructions: Sear the lamb loin chops in a hot skillet with coconut oil until browned on all sides, then set them aside.
Saute the minced shallots, lemongrass, ginger, and garlic paste in the same skillet until fragrant and caramelized.
Stir in the Malaysian curry powder and cook for one minute to bloom the spices.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer to create a thick curry base.
Line the bottom and sides of a traditional clay pot with the softened agave leaf to form a protective cooking nest.
Transfer the seared lamb chops and the rich curry sauce into the agave-lined clay pot.
Cover the clay pot tightly and bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for ninety minutes until the lamb is tender and shaggy.
Uncover the clay pot for the last fifteen minutes of cooking to allow the sauce to reduce into a jammy glaze.
